calls.key_strengths:
1. Created genuine space for Kelly to share at length — minimal interruption in first 15 min, Kelly volunteered deep context unprompted.
2. Strong "fast forward 60-90 days" anchor question revealed clear success vision.
3. Effective program link: "not carrying it alone anymore" explicitly tied to Kelly's stated helplessness.
4. Honest handling of mentor tenure objection ("I'll be completely honest... 13 months, zero mentor departures").
5. Natural, pressure-free close after rapport was established.
calls.critical_misses:
1. BOMB DROPPED, NOT CYCLED: Kelly said "I expect something bad will happen to him eventually because he doesn't want to be here" — Harry acknowledged then immediately cheerleaded ("awesome trait as a mum"). This is the biggest miss on the call.
2. No specific emotional labels using Kelly's language — "carrying a lot of weight" is generic, not Green.
3. "Feeling helpless" acknowledged but not cycled — moved to program pitch too quickly.
4. Opening slightly transactional: "purpose of this call is..." rather than genuine curiosity framing.
5. No explicit Ask Permission before going deeper.
calls.one_drill: CYCLE THE BOMB: When a parent drops an emotional bomb (suicide fear, "he doesn't want to be here"), pause 3 seconds, reflect it back, cycle 2-3 times BEFORE moving forward. Drill: Harry plays parent, rep must cycle 3× on the bomb before being allowed to continue. Do NOT acknowledge-and-move.
calls.one_line: When mum says 'I expect something bad will happen to him,' that's not background noise — that's the whole call. Everything after that is noise until you've sat in that fear with her.

calls.key_strengths: Long-standing lead (since Dec 2025) reactivated with clean BAMFAM set. Son returning from Bendigo creates natural urgency window. Start fee willingness signals genuine intent — Rye correctly identified the trigger moment.
calls.critical_misses: Financial barrier around ongoing subscription fees not addressed — only start fee resolved. No emotional pain stack in notes. Three-month gap in engagement is a trust erosion risk. Scored from rep notes only — transcript unavailable.
calls.one_drill: Pre-call prep for Monday: Rye to map Steffany's 3 fears (son's situation after Bendigo, ongoing cost, will it actually help). Prepare pain-first close with specific link-back to Koban's situation.
calls.one_line: Koban coming home is the trigger Rye has been waiting for since December — this is the close window, do not BAMFAM past it.

calls.key_strengths: Verbal close secured despite wifi connectivity issues. Creative adaptation — directed mum to complete form offline. Showed composure under difficult call conditions.
calls.critical_misses: LUCKY CLOSE — SIEL 3.7 but outcome is Onboarding Booked. Mum was likely pre-sold before the call. No emotional investment evident in notes — high churn risk if Brodie does not anchor the decision emotionally in activation call. No pain stack built.
calls.one_drill: Brodie activation priority: Call Jasmine within 24hrs, anchor the YES emotionally — ask her what made her decide today, reflect it back, build commitment consistency before W1.
calls.one_line: She said yes on a bad connection — Brodie needs to make sure she is emotionally anchored before Henley gets home, or this could reverse.

calls.key_strengths: Both parents engaged on the call — dual buy-in is rare and a strong buying signal. That both showed up is worth noting.
calls.critical_misses: Single-line notes — impossible to assess SIEL technique. No record of pain surfaced, emotional moments, or what stalled the close. Scored from rep notes only.
calls.one_drill: Partner objection-prevention frame: when dad is on the call, Bailey should name it early — address the fact that BOTH parents are hearing this together and use it as a close anchor.
calls.one_line: Dad on the call is an asset, not a blocker — use the dual parent presence to build shared commitment before BAMFAM.

calls.key_strengths: Discovered DCJ funding pathway — turned financial blocker into a structured next step. Shows resourcefulness on the financial objection.
calls.critical_misses: Notes too sparse to assess SIEL technique. No impact cycling, emotional exploration, or pain naming visible. Scored from rep notes only — transcript unavailable.
calls.one_drill: Post-call debrief habit: Bailey to write 3+ bullet notes per call capturing (1) mum pain named, (2) emotional moment, (3) where call stalled.
calls.one_line: The DCJ funding find is good instinct — but name her pain before you solve the logistics.

calls.key_strengths: 1. Strong rapport — natural small talk, name coincidence, relaxed opener. 2. Rich family context surfaced — blended family, birth mum story, ADHD/ODD, husband away. 3. Decent program pivot — linked not carrying it all on your own to her stated pain. 4. Good objection isolation — tested if Anthony said no, would you still go and got YES. 5. Tayla mentioned potentially enrolling second son Chase too.
calls.critical_misses: 1. EMOTIONAL BOMBS WALKED PAST: Birth mum abandonment, resentment, brick wall — all acknowledged and moved on. Zero cycling. 2. Program mention too early — in first 90 seconds. 3. No $111 close on the day — Tayla asked and Anthony let her off. 4. Partner objection accepted too passively. 5. No GREEN labels — every label was generic, never used her exact words.
calls.one_drill: EMOTIONAL BOMB CYCLING DRILL: Pull this transcript. Find the 5 bombs. When mum says it PAUSE. Tell me more. What does it bring up for you. Label it using her exact words. Do not move on until 2+ minutes spent on each bomb.
calls.one_line: The hardest part wasn't what she said — it was what she dropped and you walked past. The birth mum story wasn't background. That WAS the whole call.

calls.key_strengths: Strong situational discovery — complex foster care story fully uncovered. Good rapport, casual tone. Clean opening frame. Genuine empathy at key moments.
calls.critical_misses: Emotional labeling generic — never named Beck's specific emotions (exhaustion, grief, guilt). Cost objection not isolated before addressing. Husband not brought in or triaged as deal blocker. Impact not cycled back repeatedly.
calls.one_drill: Emotional label drill: for every impact moment, name the emotion — 'It sounds like you're grieving the family you hoped to be' not 'it sounds tough'.
calls.one_line: Pain over price: 'Before we talk dollars, Beck — if cost wasn't a factor, is Blues and Greens what Braxton needs right now?'

calls.key_strengths: Strong impact moment — suicide concern surfaced and reflected clearly. Clean close, 10/10 scale response handled well. Good opening frame. Natural rapport and warmth throughout.
calls.critical_misses: Emotional labeling too general — never named Judith's specific fear (terror, loss, grief). Pain stack built in rep's words not mum's own language. Suicide concern surfaced but not cycled back hard enough.
calls.one_drill: Words in her mouth drill: use Judith's own phrases in the pain stack — 'You said Jack told you he feels worthless — that's not just teenage talk, that's a boy who's silently screaming.'
calls.one_line: 'You said it yourself Judith — you're not just worried about his rugby, you're worried about him being here.'

calls.key_strengths: Strong NDIS pivot — identified and resolved funding question immediately. Good situational discovery of complex profile (autism/ADHD/ID). Warm rapport. Effective 'what if nothing changes' question.
calls.critical_misses: Emotional labeling weak — never named Leanne's fear or isolation. Pre-sold mum = coaching opportunity missed. Impact not cycled before close. Pain stack used program language not mum's words.
calls.one_drill: Easy close drill: for pre-sold mums, always test — 'What's the one thing that scares you most if we don't get Matt the right support right now?'
calls.one_line: Easy closes don't mean good calls — always find the fear before the close.

calls.key_strengths: Good casual rapport at opening. Clean frame set. Leo's interests surfaced (surfing, skateboarding). Follow-up booked for Monday.
calls.critical_misses: MASSIVE miss: Sophie disclosed personal teenage trauma — not followed up at all. Emotional labeling barely present. Impact on Sophie's daily life never explored. Pain stack empty. Went straight to $111 multiple times without addressing 'dad needs to agree'.
calls.one_drill: Gold moment drill: when a parent discloses personal history, STOP — 'Sophie, pause — you just said you had a hard time as a teenager. What happened for you? Because I think that's exactly why you're here today.'
calls.one_line: The parent's story is usually the son's story in 15 years — if she gives you hers, take it.

calls.key_strengths: Exceptional situational discovery — deepest story of all 5 calls. Objection handling excellent — offered to personally engage Tyson. 'Load lifted' closing moment shows real connection made.
calls.critical_misses: Opening frame weak/confusing. Emotional labeling generic — missed naming Jo's abandonment, grief, isolation. Impact not cycled. BAMFAM without start fee collected.
calls.one_drill: Abandonment cycling drill: 'Jo, Tyson said this morning 'if he was so worried about me, why would he leave me?' — that's a wound. Before we move on, tell me what that was like to hear.'
calls.one_line: When a 15-year-old asks why his dad left him, that's not a conversation about the program — that's the whole reason you're here. Stay there.
